coupling.summary: Dual-source persistence (electron-store + SDK service) requires every CRUD operation to write two places. The IPC pipeline (main process → preload → contextBridge → Window API → component) is the same length as the existing custom-agents pattern, but adds a new dependency chain. Type triple-divergence risk (SDK auto-generated / preload manual type / store serialized shape) with no CI bridge between packages.

debuggability.summary: Startup injection silently fails — void syncMcpWithStore() with no error handling means servers disappear after restart with zero feedback. Delete flow has .catch(() => {}) swallowing auth.remove and disconnect errors. Command string splitting breaks for quoted args silently. No structured logging, no user-facing error toasts.

convergence.summary: Option B (electron-store) correctly avoids touching the 982-line Effect service layer and follows the proven Phase 1 custom agents pattern. However, the plan was written against a stale snapshot of ipc.ts — RESERVED_STORE_NAMES was removed in commit 9e953e7be and validation helpers were moved to module level.

surface_area.summary: 8 files across 2 packages. New preload types, new Window API methods, SDK type re-exports. The ElectronAPI and Window.api type definitions are independently maintained with drift risk (getDesktopPluginConfig already exists in preload types but not app.tsx). i18n needs 18 files updated. store-keys/store-length IPC handlers lack reserved-name guard.

testability.summary: 3/5 changes fail isolation testing. IPC handlers require Electron main process. Preload bridge requires Electron renderer. DialogEditMcp requires full ContextProvider stack with no existing test fixture. Only the McpServerEntry type file is trivially testable.

error_clarity.summary: Critical gap. Silent .catch(() => {}) on disconnect and auth.remove. Silent data loss on startup injection. Command splitting failures surface as opaque MCP service errors. No span annotations, no structured logging. Pre-existing i18n gap (mcp.status.needs_client_registration key doesn't exist in en.ts).

reversibility.summary: Changes are tightly interleaved. Group A (IPC/preload) and Group C (dialog components) are entangled — reverting one breaks the other. Group B (Window API types) becomes dead code if Group C is reverted. No clean revert ordering exists.

blocking_objections.finding: Stale pattern reference: Plan says 'Add "desktop-mcp-servers" to RESERVED_STORE_NAMES in ipc.ts' but RESERVED_STORE_NAMES no longer exists in registerIpcHandlers (removed in commit 9e953e7be). Validation helpers isValidAgentDef/validateAndFilterAgents were moved to module level (lines 22-35). The plan was written against a stale version of the codebase.
blocking_objections.repair: Re-read current ipc.ts before implementation. Either add RESERVED_STORE_NAMES back as a module-level const or use the existing module-level validation pattern.

blocking_objections.finding: Additive change claim is false. The plan labels Step 4 as 'additive changes only' but requires: replacing handleRemove entirely (line 167), replacing the inline form JSX (lines 169-193), changing FormMode type (line 41), replacing startAdd/resetForm/submitAdd/handleKeyDown (lines 116-164), and replacing the submit buttons (lines 306-322). File is 81% dirty (261/323 lines). These are destructive edits affecting ~40% of the component.
blocking_objections.repair: Either acknowledge these as destructive rewrites, or land the current dirty WIP first then apply UI improvements as clean delta, or keep inline form intact and only add edit/delete-as-remove incrementally.

blocking_objections.finding: Unverified SDK API surface. Plan calls sdk.client.mcp.auth.remove({ name }) and expects configs with environment/headers/oauth shapes. Current code only uses McpRemoteConfig { type: 'remote', url, enabled }. Auth.remove and local config support may not exist in the SDK. Building features against assumed APIs.
blocking_objections.repair: Audit the actual SDK client types before implementation. If the SDK doesn't support auth.remove, local configs, or extended config fields, scope down to what the SDK provides.

weak_objections.finding: Delete flow partial-failure: confirmRemove calls disconnect → auth.remove → electron-store remove with .catch(() => {}) on each step. If auth.remove fails but disconnect succeeds, auth tokens are orphaned. If disconnect fails but store removal succeeds, zombie entry persists.
weak_objections.repair: Make electron-store removal the final step. Log each step's result. Use Promise.allSettled for partial failure reporting.

weak_objections.finding: i18n namespace inconsistency: Plan adds ~30 keys under 'dialog.mcp.*' but existing keys are under 'dialog.server.*' (e.g., 'dialog.server.menu.delete'). Translators must check two namespaces.
weak_objections.repair: Either migrate existing 'dialog.server.*' keys to 'dialog.mcp.*' or keep new keys under 'dialog.server.*'.

weak_objections.finding: Command string splitting on whitespace (split(/\s+/)) breaks for quoted arguments. A command like 'npx -y "@modelcontextprotocol/server foo"' is split into incorrect tokens.
weak_objections.repair: Accept string[] directly in the form (arg list editor) or use a shell parser (shell-quote package). At minimum, add a warning tooltip.

informational_findings.finding: OAuth clientSecret in plaintext electron-store. Plan acknowledges this as acceptable for local-only but provides no mitigation timeline.
informational_findings.repair: File follow-up issue to encrypt sensitive fields using safeStorage.encryptString(). Add a note in the UI that secrets are stored locally.

recommendation: The plan's Option B architecture (electron-store + startup injection) is fundamentally sound and converges with the Phase 1 pattern. However, three blocking issues must be repaired: (1) rewrite the plan against the actual current ipc.ts, (2) correct the 'additive change' claim for dialog-select-mcp.tsx, (3) verify the SDK API surface before implementation. Additionally, the startup injection error handling and delete-flow partial-failure handling must be strengthened before execution begins.
